title: 7 Bali's Secret Gems - Hidden Paradises - by Great Bali Villas
excerpt: Whilst travelling around Bali in recent months we have come across a list of places that we would happily classify as 7 Bali's Secret Gems. Bukit Cinta, Bunut Bolong Tree, Bukit Batu Kursi, Hidden Canyon Beji Guwang, Sunset Point Amed, Gili Putih Sumberkima, Kayu Putih. These places are the ones that we climbed, trekked to or simply just stumbled across in our travels that left us thinking. “why haven’t we ever heard of this place?” So here is our list (just our first list…for now) of hidden gems in Bali that you didn’t know existed. Because WE certainly didn’t!
body: Whilst travelling around Bali in recent months we have come across a list of places that we would happily classify as hidden gems in Bali. These places are the ones that we climbed, trekked to or simply just stumbled across in our travels that left us thinking. “why haven’t we ever heard of this place?” So here is our list (just our first list…for now) of hidden gems in Bali that you didn’t know existed. Because WE certainly didn’t!

Bukit Cinta was something that we found on google maps when we were actually searching for another Bukit in the same area. This is a place that we really only drove to just to fill in a few hours of our afternoon. We are so glad we did!

Bukit Cinta is located in the Karangasem Regency of East Bali, and you may have seen photos of it already on Instagram and wondered, “where is that?” Once we arrived and saw the location and the view ourselves, we were left nodding stupidly and saying things like, “oh yeah, I recognize this place.”
To find Bukit Cinta, just head to East Bali and the Karangasem area and punch it into Google Maps. Or just click here, and we have done it for you.

Bunut Bolong tree is located in the village of Manggisari in Jembrana, West Bali. This is one of our true hidden gems in Bali! It is a huge Banyan Tree, and is unique due to the fact that it has a road going right through a hole in the middle of it. The hole is almost entirely natural, and has only been widened slightly to allow safer passage of vehicles.
To find it, head toward Gilimanuk and take the turn toward north Bali. You can find the tree on Google Maps here.

If you are anywhere up in the North West of Bali, then Bukit Batu Kursi is worth checking out. Located in Pemuteran, it is the place to go if you are interested in checking out the fantastic views of the area.

Bukit Batu Kursi is one you might have to work a little to reach. The carpark is very easy to find, but to reach the top you must walk, and that walk involves a lot of concrete stairs up the side of a good sized hill. Don’t worry though, the hike only takes around 30 minutes.

To find Bukit Batu Kursi turn off the main road through Pemuteran onto Jalan Pura Pemuteran and park in front of the large temple at the end of the road. Pay a small donation, sign the visitors log and off you go!
This is literally a Hidden gem in Bali. The proof is in the name!

In truth, Hidden Canyon is not exactly hidden, it’s just that not too many people know that it exists. Situated in the Gianyar Region, it is not too far north of Sanur and very close to Tegenungan Waterfall.

You will pay a higher entrance fee at Hidden Canyon than you would at a waterfall, but it is entirely worth it. With the fee you get a guide, which is essential because this is actually an adventure trek.
To find Hidden Canyon Beji Guwang, you can come from Ubud or Sanur. It is easy to find from anywhere in southern Bali.
For anyone that has been to Amed, this is not exactly a hidden location in Bali. For those that have not, you are the reason Sunset Point has made the list.

There is nothing so special about this place, except the view and, of course, the sunsets. If you have ever seen a beautiful photo on Instagram or Facebook of a sunset in Bali, with the sun setting alongside or over the top of Mount Agung, then it is a good bet that the photo was taken from this location!

Not too much more can be said for this one. To find it, drive along the coast road through Amed until you find the Sunset Point Restaurant. Find yourself a table before sunset, order a drink, sit back and enjoy.
My six Hidden Gem in Bali is one that not many know about….yet. Gili Putih Sumberkima is a small sand island that is located just off the coast of Pemuteran, in north west Bali.

This thin little strip of sand has seen a bit of love on Instagram of late, with some very nice drone shots taken of it at sunset. As you can imagine, it is very picturesque, and the view actually FROM the island of the nearby coast and mountains of Bali is just as good.

There is not much to do on Gili Putih Sumberkima, but then that is sort of the point. The island has a swing (for photos) and a couple of Bale style huts to rest from the sun inside. Other than that, it is sand and shells for as far as you can walk. Bring your towel, lay down and rest on the most peaceful beach you will find. This really is the typical desert island. The best part is, it’s only about 20 minutes by boat from the mainland.
For a Google location on Gili Putih Sumberkima, click here.

title: 8 Best Gym's in Bali
excerpt: When dreaming of the tropical island paradise Bali and its beautiful beaches and ever so mouthwatering foodie scene, one thing might not come to your mind: working out. However, laying by your private villa pool is only fun for so long and you sadly cannot eat 24/7, so why not treat your body and mind and go for a workout in Bali? […]
body: When dreaming of the tropical island paradise Bali and its beautiful beaches and ever so mouthwatering foodie scene, one thing might not come to your mind: working out. However, laying by your private villa pool is only fun for so long and you sadly cannot eat 24/7, so why not treat your body and mind and go for a workout in Bali?

Skip the holiday laziness and choose your favourite from the vast array of gym options Bali has to offer to get your sweat on. We promise, your favourite smoothie bowl is going to taste even better after kicking goals in one of the best gyms in Bali!
Address: Jl. Petitenget 8L, SeminyakContact: info@motionfitnessbali.com – +62 812 4627 9322Opening Hours: Mo – Fr 7:30am – 7:30pm, Sat 8:30 am – 12:00pmInstagram: @motionfitnessbali

This Seminyak gym is popular amongst locals, expats and tourist alike. Join them for one of their famed classes. Whether you want to form your BrazilianBoom or try Triple Threat – the passionate fitness experts will make sure you leave sweaty but happy.

If you are into a more traditional gym workout in Bali – barre, TRX, kettle bells, dumbbells, resistance bands, balls, foot weights, pilates rings will have you covered. Why not book one of their trainers for a private session at your Seminyak villa?

Spread over an area of about 2.500 sq meters, you will find everything your sports loving heart is looking for at this gym in Seminyak. Stylishly furnished in harmonious and peaceful surroundings, world-class wellness facilities and services can be found to ensure you’ll leave exhausted but happy. From yoga to spa treatments and personal training sessions as well as classes such as MMA or capoeira, you will get to enjoy an array of lifestyle experiences with access to all their wellness facilities.
Address: Jalan Nakula, SeminyakContact: +62 811 388661Opening Hours: Mo – Sa 6AM – 10PM, Sun 8am – 6pm

Venture to Hammerhead Gym located just off Sunset Road in Seminyak for a full-body workout. The most complete gym in Bali offers more than 30 workout stations with world leading brands in a large air-conditioned space. Shaping your body has never been more motivating in this inspiring surrounding with the support of their highly experienced trainers. Maybe you’ll spot some of their award-winning competitors!
If you like a mix of classes and free workouts – join them for their daily Zumba class and stock up on your supplements from their large selection of high quality brands.
Address: Jalan Canggu Club, CangguContact: +62 361 8483939Opening Hours: Daily, 6am – 10pmInstagram: @cangguclub

Training at Bali’s famed Canggu Club not only includes a well-equipped gym – the Canggu Club Day Pass or Membership, it also offers you direct access to some of Bali’s most thrilling entertainment and sporting venues such as Splash Water Park, a 25m lap pool and a fully equipped tennis centre.

You won’t be missing anything during working up a sweat at this Bali gym in Canggu, whether you join their fitness class program with international trainers or prefer a workout in their large air-conditioned gym consisting of state-of-the-art cardio and fitness machines, free weights and stretching. Treat yourself to a treatment at Bodytemple, one of Bali’s best spas, afterwards!
Address: Jl. Raya Semat, Canggu & Jalan Segara Wangi #36, JimbaranContact: info@s2scrossfit.comOpening Hours: Mon – Fri 6:30am-6:30pm, Sat 7am-12noon, Sun closedInstagram: @s2scrossfit_bali
Strength to strength! Being the first CrossFit affiliate in Bali, you’ll be in good hands whether you want to try CrossFit in Bali for the first time or simply can’t go without it while on holiday!

Offering drop-in classes to monthly memberships at their two outlets in Canggu and Jimbaran as well as a complete Eat Sleep Liftpackage offering accommodation, daily breakfast and unlimited access to their CrossFit classes and open gym. Join them for a session of beach volleyball, basketball, tennis or soccer to stay active in tropical Bali. Must-join: their beach training!
Contact: 2befitbali@gmail.comInstagram: @2befitbali

Priding themselves as the #1 beach bootcamp specialists on the island with years of experience in organising the best beach workouts, you almost cannot miss out on joining one of their famed beach-side bootcamps in the early mornings. And what’s better than a refreshing dip on the ocean and a coconut on the beach after working up a sweat along a motivated international group!
Address: No.5, Banjar Nyuh Kuning, Jalan Nyuh Bulan, UbudContact: ubud@movementmatters-bali.comOpening Hours: Daily, 7am – 7pmInstagram: @movementmattersbali

If you are after a workout different to the usual weight lifting, you should pay a visit to Movement Matters gym while staying Ubud. They offer a large variation of pilates (Mat, Reformer, Cadillac, Stability Chair, Barrels, Wedge, EXO Chair, MOTR®) and Gyrotonic (movement of the ‘fascia’ – think of moving the web-like structure in your body) in private, private duet and small group classes.

title: The Best Spa's in Bali
excerpt: 1. MANDALA SPA at THE VILLAS BY BISMA EIGHT Address: The Villas at Bisma Eight. Jl. Bisma, Ubud Opening hours: Daily, 9 am to 9 pm Bookings: +62 361 908 8888 Re-align and balance your energies at Mandala SPA, a place to relax and recharge in the luxurious Villas at Bisma Eight. Be treated to a contemporary spa […]
body: Address: The Villas at Bisma Eight. Jl. Bisma, Ubud

Re-align and balance your energies at Mandala SPA, a place to relax and recharge in the luxurious Villas at Bisma Eight. Be treated to a contemporary spa experience amongst Ubud’s magical jungle town, offering a range of rejuvenating and restorative treatments Mandala SPA is a delight to travellers.

Essential oils are handcrafted locally, select an aromatherapy blend of your choice for your treatment or begin at the scrub bar with an organic rice base, then add from a variety of ingredients to create your ideal blend. Indulge in their Signature Balinese Massage which releases tension and toxins, or the iconic Temple Floral Aromatherapy which nurtures and calms the mind – a visit to Mandala SPA is a spa experience not forgotten.
Address: Mambal, Abiansemal, Badung Regency

Treat yourself with five-star pampering and ultimate relaxation at Fivelements Wellness, Spa located in the magical jungle of Ubud. It uniquely combines the traditional Balinese healing rituals with innovative wellness concepts, all the while using natural and superfood ingredients for a beautified glow inside and out.

Bathe in lemons, oranges and pandan leaves overlooking the peaceful Ayung River as part of the 2-hour Sakti Ritual or embark on a healing journey to nurture your heart, mind and soul. Either way, we guarantee that you’ll leave this place feeling refreshed and renewed

Feel at one with nature with a range of heavenly spa treatments at Maya Ubud, their spa situated amongst the lush treetops of the jungle. Rejuvenate and escape amongst the tropics with their signature massage, the Soothing Maya Massage, an indulgent treatment of long massage strokes paired perfectly with fragrant essential oils.

Also on offer alongside a range of next-level beauty treatments is their iconic oxygen facials, perfect for refreshing dull and tired skin. Guests can also opt for A Morning at the Riverside spa package, including a healthy set lunch for 2, a soothing Maya Massage and a foot massage, all whilst relaxing by the riverside.
Opening hours: Daily, 11 am until 4.30 pm

Located in the small traditional village of Payogan, 4 kilometres west of Ubud, the Ubud Traditional Spa offers you a choice of four uniquely delivered massages to provide you with the best in Balinese relaxation. Immerse yourself in the spa’s sustainable infrastructure with its traditional Balinese furnishings and use of reclaimed wood while you treat yourself like royalty with a Royal Massage or their Balinese Reflexology for those worn feet.

Pop next door to the historic Pura Pucak temple – active since the eleventh century! – on your way in or take a hike along the famous Champuhan Ridge trekking route. Complimentary return transportation will be provided to their guests in the Ubud area.
Address: Jl. Dewi Sri No 88E Kelurahan Legian
Opening hours: Monday – Saturday, 9.30 am – 18.30 pm

Wherever you’re staying in Bali, a visit to Lumina Aesthetics in Seminyak should be on your bucket list. It’s is the latest clinical spa to open in Bali bringing a new breed of anti-aging clinical treatments, including one of the widest range of FDA approved lasers, ultrasound and radio frequency aesthetic technology.

Lumina Aesthetics is set over three floors, comprising of laser rooms, clinical and spa rooms. Their unique and diverse international team will assist you in all your skin needs, including a consultation with one of their experienced doctors who have in-depth anatomical understanding of the treatments to achieve the best possible results.
Address: Jalan Taman Ganesha No. 9 Petitenget, Kerobokan, Bali 80361 Opening hours: Daily, 9 am – 10 pmBookings: +62 361 3021 888

Make the most of your tropical getaway with a visit to one of the best spas in Bali at Spa Alila. Offering guests heartfelt, homely treatments that will pamper you silly, we promise that you will leave feeling re-energised.

Sourcing their ingredients only locally, fresh produce is made into lush treatments for your weary soul. If you are vacationing with a special someone, spa together with Spa Alila’s couple package which comes complete with refreshments overlooking the brilliant sunset and a romantic dinner. Or, spoil yourself with a Mineral Ritual which offers a deep acupressure reflexology treatment, microbubble bath and facial. It tops off with a three-course healthy lunch at Alila Seminyak’s Seasalt by the coast.
Address: Jalan Petitenget No. 7, Seminyak

Sundari Day Spa practices traditional healing methods with a modern twist, they use natural products which are iconic for their beautifying and detoxifying properties – promising absolute relaxation and rejuvenation. Treat yourself to a Sundari Signature Massage, an in-house created treatment that combines the best out of several massage techniques for the ultimate spa experience in Seminyak.

title: The Best Cafes in Bali
excerpt: 1. CAFE ORGANIC Cuisine: Health food, plant-based, vegetarian with vegan & gluten-free optionsAddress: Jalan Petitenget No. 99X, Seminyak & Jalan Umalas 1Contact: +62 821 4648 9669Opening Hours: Daily, 7am – 10pmInstagram: @cafeorganicbali One of Bali’s most iconic eateries, Cafe Organic offers health-conscious vegetarian and vegan meals passionately crafted using fresh, locally- farmed produce. Wood grains and distressed whites dominate the space […]
body: Cuisine: Health food, plant-based, vegetarian with vegan & gluten-free optionsAddress: Jalan Petitenget No. 99X, Seminyak & Jalan Umalas 1Contact: +62 821 4648 9669Opening Hours: Daily, 7am – 10pmInstagram: @cafeorganicbali

One of Bali’s most iconic eateries, Cafe Organic offers health-conscious vegetarian and vegan meals passionately crafted using fresh, locally- farmed produce. Wood grains and distressed whites dominate the space inviting a minimalist Scandinavian feel. Every corner has been styled for the gram with the star of the show being the açai bowls served in coconuts.

At their Petitenget location in the buzzing heart of Seminyak, you’ll find marble table tops, living green walls, and the oh so famous #GardenGangstas mural. Their latest endeavour in Umalas is a wide open space set in Bali’s rice terraces and veggie gardens. Whether you want to sit back for an afternoon taro latte and get serenaded by jazzy tunes or meet up for a big brunch with the crew, this is your rendezvous.
Cuisine: Modern AustralianAddress: Jalan Lb. Sari No. 8, Kerobokan, Seminyak Contact: +62 812 4601 0515Opening Hours: Daily, 7.30am – 6pmInstagram: @coffeecartelbali

Coffee Cartel is everything you’re looking for – delicious food, specialty coffee, gluten-free, vegetarian and vegan options, Insta-grammable aesthetics, fast Wi-Fi, and spacious seating areas. Take your pick of sitting at their large wooden table indoors, beautiful marble tables outdoors or upstairs on their al-fresco terrace and settle in to enjoy their vast menu of mouth-watering dishes.

Make sure you try the Vegan Smak Hotcakes with candy floss and Banana Nice-Cream, or the Bali Bowl with all your favourite Balinese Nasi Campur sides in a modern salad. Also, don’t forget to get yourself a Ripple printed latte to add to those aesthetically pleasing Instagram flat lays!
Cuisine: Western & MexicanAddress: Jalan Kayu Aya No. 22A, Seminyak Contact: +62 361 738667 Opening Hours: Daily, 7.30am – 10pm Instagram: @seacircus

You can’t miss this rainbow coloured Seminyak institution with its quirky interiors and mouth-watering day-to-night food offerings. You’ll have a hard time choosing a menu favourite between its famous Acai Bowl, Fish Tacos and Margarita jugs as Sea Circus simply has one of the tastiest selections in town for breakfast, lunch and dinner.
Bring the family for an addictive breakfast experience or stop by with your love for a non-pretentious feast under the glittering fairy lights.
Cuisine: Healthy & flavoursomeAddress: Jalan Drupadi 1 No. 2A, SeminyakBookings: info@sheltercafebali.comOpening hours: Daily, 7.30am – 6pmInstagram: @shelterseminyak

The breezy rooftop cafe stands as one of Seminyak’s most popular hangouts for great food, delicious coffee, power-packed healthy smoothies and fresh juices all wrapped up in a relaxing atmosphere. A must-visit in Seminyak when you’re looking for one of the best cafes in Bali and the ultimate healthy yet satisfying meal!
Cuisine: VeganAddress: Jalan Petitenget No. 12XContact: +62 859 3112 0209Opening Hours: Daily, 6am – 10pmInstagram: @kyndcommunity

Kynd has quickly risen to become one of Bali’s most popular cafes, combining uber Instagramable interiors with delicious vegan cuisine and stellar service. Their inventive and 100% plant-based menu is designed to show you just how good veggies really can taste and will have you coming back for more!

Visit them early in the morning for delicious coconut MYLK coffees, waffles and customised Berry Tropical smoothie bowls or come by in the evening to try their mind-blowing dinner menu and super cute cocktails! PS: Save room for dessert and indulge in their plant-based ice-creams, milkshakes and freak shakes at their Kynd Creamery located next door!

Cuisine: Australian breakfast, Asian inspired tapas, tacos & mainsAddress: Corner of Jalan Kayu Aya & Jalan Kayu JatiBookings: +62 813 5379 4644Opening hours: Daily, 8am – 11pmInstagram: @neonpalmsbali

Located on the second level above the unmissable boutique store Bali Boat Shed is the latest masterstroke of the culinary minds behind Seminyak institution Sea Circus. With its striking tropical decor, incredible cocktails & delectable menu, Neon Palms cannot be missed on your Seminyak adventures!

Indulge in their signature Flatbread Tacos, Korean Spiced Chicken Strips and King Prawn Sushi Bowl for the ultimate Bali feast. Open for breakfast, lunch & dinner you can shop, wine and dine all under the same jungle- styled roof – don’t blame us if you go on a spending spree after a few cocktails!
Cuisine: Premium coffee, modern Australian lunch & dinnerAddress: Jl. Tanah Barak No.6, CangguOpening Hours: Daily, 7am – 5pmInstagram: @blacklistcoffee.bali

Blacklist Coffee Roasters is an award-winning coffee roastery that was founded in Perth, Western Australia, before setting up shop in the iconic Canggu. Coffee enthusiasts can look forward to good coffee, good food and good times at this sophisticated roastery, restaurant and cafe, whilst True coffee connoisseurs can up the ante with the #BlacklistCoffeeTasting experience, offering a unique coffee degustation journey.

Stop by Blacklist Coffee Roasters for breakfast or lunch daily and enjoy specialty coffee, signature cocktails, craft beer and premium wine, served alongside a refined menu of modern Australian cuisine.

title: 3 Places to Get Unique Souvenir in Bali
excerpt: “Shopping is one of the mandatory agenda that commonly carried out by tourists when traveling to Bali. The choice of place also varies. However, it is incomplete if you don’t visit traditional markets in Bali that sell various Balinese souvenirs. Not only because of their affordable prices, but also because these markets provide the wide […]
body: Sukawati is the center of arts crafts in Bali. It is common knowledge that the Sukawati art market is a paradise of unique souvenirs and handicrafts. The Sukawati art market is very famous for selling traditional Balinese clothes and souvenirs at very cheap prices. so you can buy Balinese souvenirs and clothing such as Balinese ornamental Batik which you can give to your friends and family, without spending a lot on vacation expenses.
Location : Jalan Raya Sukawati, Sukawati, Kec. Sukawati, Kabupaten Gianyar, BaliOpen : Daily from 09 AM to 06 PM

Your vacation is not complete if you haven’t visited Ubud district. Ubud is a tourism area which is known to have a complete tourist attraction, the traditional market in Ubud Gianyar is one of the best markets in Bali and is well known to foreign countries. This is the place where many traders sell Balinese arts and crafts items such as Balinese sandals, Balinese clothes, mats, paintings, sculptures, unique mirrors to key chains at very cheap prices.
Location : Jl. Raya Ubud No.35, Ubud, Kecamatan Ubud, Kabupaten Gianyar, Bali

Its location in the heart of Denpasar city makes this market the choice of tourists who want to shop for Balinese souvenirs but don’t have much time to get around, because the time needed to go to this market is only about 30 minutes from Ngurah Rai International Airport. This market has a wide selection of souvenirs that can be taken home, ranging from clothes, accessories, paintings, sculptures, and complete traditional ornaments available at this art market located on Gajah Mada street, Denpasar.
Location : Jl. Gajah Mada, Pemecutan, Kec. Denpasar Barat, Kota Denpasar, Bali

title: 5 Best Beach Club in Bali
excerpt: “Bali is home to some of the best beach clubs in the world. There’s something for everyone from parties with international DJ’s, luxurious or laid-back spaces to spend the day relaxing by the beach, chill sunset drinks, and family-friendly beach clubs“ 1. KU DE TA Set foot into KU DE TA, Bali’s iconic sunset destination and pioneer […]
body: Set foot into KU DE TA, Bali’s iconic sunset destination and pioneer in the beach club scene. Immerse yourself in the ultimate beachfront experience with handcrafted cocktails, eclectic summer beats and a fiery sunset across the horizon. The restaurant, bar and beach lounge serves up epic grazing plates, fresh meals and delicious cocktail concoctions with fabulous views out to the beach.
Location : Jalan Kayu Aya No. 9, Seminyak, Kuta, Kab. Badung, Bali

Located across the street from Padma Beach in Legian, Azul Beach club will catch your eye with its bamboo structure that makes it look like a multi-level beach hut. A great place to come to watch the sunset in Legian, have a cocktail and some bites to eat. Their sunset pods offer the best views but do have a minimum spend. Azul Beach Clubis also home to Bali’s first Tiki bar, has a pool area too and staff here were super friendly.
Location : Jl. Padma No.2, Legian, Kuta, Kab. Badung, Bali

Comprising three restaurants, three bars, an infinity pool and a spacious lawn area, Potato Head beach club boasts spectacular views out to the glistening Indian Ocean. The unique open-air amphitheatre structure has been designed by Indonesian architect Andra Matin, and features colourful antique shutters reclaimed from across Indonesia.
Location : Jalan Petitenget No. 51B, Seminyak, Kec Kuta Utara, Kab. Badung, Bali

Bringing a new era of beach club chic to the island, Finns VIP Beach Club boasts luxury VIP service, exclusive guest benefits, premium dining options, and stunning sunset and ocean views. A cut above the average Bali beach club, Finns VIP Beach Club invites you to experience the essence of beachfront luxe during your vacation in the tropics.
Location : Jalan Pantai Berawa, Canggu, Kec. Kuta Utara, Kab Badung, Bali

Elevate your Bali experience at OMNIA, a jewel perched amongst the clifftops hundreds of metres above Uluwatu’s stunning coastline. Relax and unwind poolside at their stylish day club or admire sweeping Indian Ocean views from your own floating daybed. This is a one-of-a-kind, day-into-night party experience straight out of your Instagram’s Explore feed.
Location : Jl. Belimbing Sari, Pecatu, Kec. Kuta Selatan, Kab. Badung, Bali

title: The Best Restaurants in Seminyak
excerpt: Bali has come a long way since Nasi Goreng and Ikan Bakar. The island buzzes with bustling venues, reservation waitlists, and award-winning chefs. The Asia Collective is a social butterfly and foodie at heart – we’re always out and about in town to discover the best spots you need to add to your must-visit-list! Indulge […]
body: Bali has come a long way since Nasi Goreng and Ikan Bakar. The island buzzes with bustling venues, reservation waitlists, and award-winning chefs.

The Asia Collective is a social butterfly and foodie at heart – we’re always out and about in town to discover the best spots you need to add to your must-visit-list! Indulge in the finest dishes from all over the world in the trendiest hotspots while enjoying some of the world’s finest cocktails.
We are spilling our secrets of places to-be-and-be-seen in our post popular dining guide – Seminyak’s Best Restaurants!
CUISINE Plant-based IndonesianADDRESS Jl. Petitenget No.51B, Seminyak (Location Map)OPENING HOURS Mon – Sun 6pm – 12amINSTAGRAM @tanamanrestaurant

Tanaman is an extraordinary dining experience rooted in a respect for plants that elevates them to the divine. With her magical hands and dazzling sense of creativity, chef Dommy Hammond honours each humble ingredient, root to fruit, and metamorphs Indonesia’s lush, tropical flora and botanicals into their full edible potential, not a single cell wasted. At its heart, Tanaman is a multi-sensory restaurant from the future where diners are invited on a cosmic plant-fueled trip through provocative plates and spirited drinks.

Each meal is inspired by one of the five elements: air, fire, earth, water and space, and draws only from a bounty of plants, along with a wild selection of mushrooms, for dishes whose components are unrecognizable in the vibrant presentations. Dehydration and fermentation, infusion, marination and smoke, there is no end to what chef Dom will try. Paired with an equally thoughtful cocktail, each course is presented along with detailed storytelling as they explain not only the fantastical end result but the journey each element took along the way.
CUISINE Latin AmericanADDRESS Jalan Petitenget No. 14BOOK A TABLE +62 812 3999INSTAGRAM @barbacoabali

Step into this large warehouse space with a private terrace overlooking Bali’s rice paddies and be instantly inspired by the mosaic-tiled floors, high ceilings, vintage leather banquettes and loft-style setting. BARBACOA’s kitchen is dedicated to BBQ, woodfire & charcoal. These combined fire methods have been influenced by Latin-American style flavours to create rustic dishes which attract a food-savvy crowd. The trendy setting features a centre bar creating the perfect atmosphere for a group dinner and pre-party drinks for your super cool crowd. The warm, inviting décor and top-class service makes Barbacoa one of the best restaurants in Seminyak.
CUISINE Fine dining Asian fusionADDRESS Jalan Kayu Aya No. 9BOOK A TABLE +62 361 736969INSTAGRAM @mejekawi

KU DE TA’s cutting-edge restaurant MEJEKAWI, means ‘sacred table’ andis an intimate space offering a sophisticated yet relaxed fine dining experience. Impressive culinary skills of British Head Chef Stephen Moore brings forward an ultracontemporary tasting menu prepared with fresh seasonal produce, which can also be paired with premium wines. Dishes are prepared with a combination of traditional Indonesian recipes and techniques, and modern cooking methods, served against a spectacular backdrop of the Indian Ocean. Sip on a refreshing aperitif prior to your meal, go for a Classic Negroni, a sparkling Bellini or a refreshing Aperol spritz – cheers!
CUISINE ItalianADDRESS Jalan Petitenget No. 170BOOK A TABLE + 62 361 9348523INSTAGRAM @damariabali

Escape to Italy with an authentic Italian food and wine experience all set within captivating interiors featuring that Capri feeling in the heart of Seminyak. The hyped Italian Osteria attracts a hip crowd and has quickly evolved into the latest place to see and be seen and has quickly become one of the best restaurants in Seminyak. Bringing the Amalfi Coast to Bali, DA MARIAhighlights the diversity of Italian food, wine, music, art and friends, with stunning interiors by Roman architects Lazzarini Pickering. Join the crowds at their busy bar and enjoy La Dolce Vita with Da Maria’s Campari-based blends for a buzzing night out.
CUISINE Italian contemporaryADDRESS Jalan Petitenget No. 100BOOK A TABLE +62 817 776 177INSTAGRAM @mauri_restaurant

Step foot into the world of Southern Italy with MAURI’s dreamy interiors of whitewashed walls and deep green cacti adorned throughout. Inspired by award-winning Chef Maurizio Bombini’s Pugliese origins, MAURI offers a contemporary Italian dining experience with a touch of tradition where ingredients are handled with care to showcase their purest flavours. Aiming to use as many homegrown items as possible from its hydroponic rooftop garden and selecting the highest quality of local products in collaboration with Balinese farmers, the restaurant offers a conscious cuisine that respects the ingredients’ seasonality and origin. For a private tasting with Chef Maurizio choose the 4-seat chef’s counter.
CUISINE IndonesianADDRESS Jalan Petitenget No. 51BOOK A TABLE +62 361 300 7990INSTAGRAM @kaumrestaurant

KAUM, which means “tribe” in Indonesian, is part of the iconic Potato Head Family and serves up authentic Indonesian cuisine in a beautiful setting. Its eclectic menu has been thoughtfully curated applying traditional indigenous cooking skills, methods and native ingredients, derived from extensive research across the archipelago. The focused menu features time-honoured recipes and traditional Balinese flavours, such as fragrant Sate Babi Kecap and Nasi Goreng. The dining space mirrors Indonesian craftsmanship, design and culture, featuring genuine Torajan wood carvings, bespoke ceramic tableware and a long communal table, re-enforcing the traditional family-style dinner setting.
CUISINE Sustainable seafoodADDRESS Jalan Petitenget No. 51BOOK A TABLE +62 361 473 7979INSTAGRAM @potatoheadbali

IJEN is the first zero-waste seafood restaurant in Indonesia, proudly serving fresh, line-caught fish in an open-air environment built from recycled materials. With sweeping views of the Indian Ocean, it’s the ideal spot for a tropical-island meal or a carefully crafted sunset cocktail. The daily catch is cooked over a wood fire and complemented by a range of creative plant-based dishes, such as a chilled watermelon salad and smoky prawn skewers, all designed for sharing. Every detail at Ijen has been consciously designed to reflect Potato Head Family’s “Good Times, Do Good” ethos.
CUISINE Western & MexicanADDRESS Jalan Kayu Aya No. 22ABOOK A TABLE +62 361 738667INSTAGRAM @seacircus

You can’t miss this rainbow coloured Seminyak institution with its quirky interiors and mouth-watering day-to-night food offerings. You’ll have a hard time choosing a menu favourite between its famous Acai Bowl, Fish Tacos and Margarita jugs as Sea Circus simply has one of the tastiest selections in town for breakfast, lunch and dinner.

title: Top 5 Beaches in Bali
excerpt: “Bali is blessed with some of the very best beaches in the world, and we’ve taken the liberty to explore them all, so pack your sunscreen and your fave bikini, wrap up your sarong and prep your hottest summer playlist, holiday mode is officially on!“ 1. Balangan Beach Balangan Beach is a white sandy beach stretch 200 […]
body: Balangan Beach is a white sandy beach stretch 200 meters with panoramic view of the Indian Ocean and blessed by the spectacular sunset that creates the romantic nuance for every visitor who visits this beach. This beach is also endowed by enormous waves that is demanded by all surfers in particular for experience up to professional surfers.
Location : Pantai Balangan, Kec. Kuta Selatan, Kab. Badung, Bali

A seemingly endless stretch of powder white perfection, Nusa Dua Beach is so flawless, it almost looks manmade. It borders the coastline of Nusa Dua’s resort area, fringed by manicured lawns and five-star hotels that have been tastefully sheltered by palm trees and natural building materials. It’s the ideal destination for a fun-filled family day out that won’t leave the little ones hot and bothered, thanks to plenty of places to cool off and recharge.
Location : Benoa, Kec. Kuta Selatan, Kab. Badung, Bali

Situated on the northeastern coast of Bali, Amed most beautiful feature is found beneath the translucent turquoise water. A healthy garden of coral lines the ocean floor, rewarding visitors with a spectacular show of vibrant colors and unique ocean life. As a result of this thriving aquatic environment, diving and snorkeling top the list of things to do at this black-sand oasis. A historical shipwreck only adds to the mystical allure.

Echo Beach is one of Bali’s most popular surf spots. The nickname overshadows its local name ‘Pantai Batu Mejan’, which takes after a significant Balinese Hindu sea temple on site. Sharing the serene rice field-landscape region of Cangguwith other wave-riding spots such as Berawa, Seseh and Pererenan, Echo Beach has become most popular due to its advanced reef breaks, 180-degree seascape and sunsets, as well as delicious seafood cafes set on a high vantage point.
Location : Jalan Pura Batu Mejan, Canggu,Kab. Badung, Bali

Pandawa Beach is a picture perfect Bali beach with beautiful white sand and sparkling clear water. The shore of the beach stretches for just over half a mile along the island’s south coast of BukitPeninsula and is located between the popular tourist areas of Nusa Dua and Uluwatu. Though often called a “hidden beach” for its location behind a wall of large limestone cliffs, it has become a famous tourist spot over the last decade. Pandawa Beach is very much worth a day trip if you are visiting Bali, as it is a wonderful example of the best that the island’s beaches have to offer.
Location : Jalan Pantai Pandawa, Kutuh, Kab. Badung, Bali

title: 5 Temples Have to See in Bali
excerpt: As a tourist destination, Bali has many attractions, one of which is Balinese art and culture. One form of Balinese art and culture is manifested in the form of a temple. Several temples in Bali have even become icons of Bali tourism which display the beauty and unique architecture of buildings from ancient heritage. Most of the Hindu temples, apart from offering architectural beauty, also have a unique location.
body: The biggest temple and the most sacred by Balinese Hindus. The location of the Besakih temple is 900 meters from the slopes of Mount Agung. Pura Besakih is a large temple complex where there are lots of other temples inside. Because of the large area of ​​the Besakih temple, it will take more than one day to explore all areas of the temple. Tourists who visit Besakih temple usually only visit the Penataran Agung temple. Pura Penataran Agung is the main and largest temple in the area of ​​the Besakih temple complex which is dedicated to the worship of Lord Brahma, Lord Shiva and Lord Vishnu.
Location : Jl. Gunung Mas, Ds, Besakih, Rendang, Kab. Karangasem, Bali

Tanah Lot Temple is built on a large coral rock located in an offshore area. To enter the temple area, it can only be passed at low tide. Meanwhile, at high tide, the Tanah Lot temple will be seen in the middle of the sea. The best time to visit Tanah Lot temple is in the afternoon before sunset. Because you will be able to see the sunset view with the silhouette of the Tanah Lot temple.
Location : Beraban, Kediri, Kab. Tabanan, Bali

If you have any questions, what are the names of the famous temples in Bali that are built on the tip of a rock, apart from the Tanah Lot temple, the Uluwatu temple is also built on the tip of the rock. Uluwatu Temple, also known as Pura Luhur Uluwatu, one of the six main Hindu temples in Bali. Apart from the uniqueness of the Uluwatu temple architecture, the attraction is also the location of the temple, which is at the end of a very steep cliff. Coral cliffs have a height of approximately 70 meters above sea level.
Location : Pecatu, Kec. Kuta Selatan, Kab. Badung, Bali

Tirta Empul means holy spring. Because in the Tirta Empul temple area there are natural springs. Water flowed to the outer area of ​​the Tirta Empul temple which is in the form of a bath with a water shower. Natural springs and baths in the temple area, which are the main attraction of the Tirta Empul temple as a vacation spot in Bali. Adjacent to the location of the Tirta Empul temple, precisely on the hill there is the Tampak Siring presidential palace. For Balinese Hindus, water from the shower at the Tirta Empul temple is holy water which is believed to bring purity, calm and good luck.
Location : Jl. Tirta, Manukaya, Tampaksiring, Kab. Gianyar, Bali

Goa Lawah is one of the most important temples for Hindus in Bali. The uniqueness of Goa Lawah temple is because the location of the temple is built in front of the cave area which is inhabited by thousands of bats. Even though there are lots of bats, you don’t need to worry about visiting Goa Lawah temple, because it is very safe. Goa Lawah Temple is located in Klungkung district to be precise in Pesinggahan village, Dawan sub-district. To the south of the location of Goa Lawah Temple there is a black sand beach and from the beach you can see Nusa Penida Island.
Location : Jl. Raya Goa Lawah, Pesinggahan, Kec. Dawan, Kab. Klungkung, Bali

title: 5 Reasons Why a Villa is Better Than a Hotel
excerpt: Nearly every villa has their own personal team dedicated to enhancing your stay. Depending on the villa these might include chef, butler and even private driver. With this kind of service you’ll be looked after in style so you can enjoy the other comforts of a relaxing holiday. Make sure you check the villa information to see what wonderful extras are included when it comes to staff and service by Great bali Villas.
body: Once previously only an option for the more affluent traveller and holiday goers, the idea of staying in a personal villa while is becoming more common all round. With a vast amount of options available, at some very appealing prices, the luxury experience of staying in your own private getaway has become the staple of holiday vacation rentals.

Within a villa you’ll get your own personal hideaway with no other’s to invade your space. Unlike a hotel you don’t need to worry about any rowdy neighbours, people taking up the sun-beds or late night (or early morning) noises. You’ll have your own private haven where you and your loved ones have absolute privacy to enjoy your holiday as you intend.

You can tailor your villa to the location you prefer – whether that be close to the beach, away from the hustle and bustle of the busy areas or even closer to your favourite hangouts. The location of a villa can be a deal breaker for most people, as what’s available to keep you entertained locally is an important factor in any person’s vacation.

Unlike hotels that can feel very “one use” stay, most villas are set up to be a home away from home. You’ll find these properties to be kitted out for all your needs, with a kitchen, lounges and own private pools. You can relax in luxury with all of the items needed to make your next vacation as simple as needed.

Nearly every villa has their own personal team dedicated to enhancing your stay. Depending on the villa these might include chef, butler and even private driver. With this kind of service you’ll be looked after in style so you can enjoy the other comforts of a relaxing holiday. Make sure you check the villa information to see what wonderful extras are included when it comes to staff and service.

Like many things in life the defining factor can always come down to price. However, by the time you’ve paid for a larger group to stay in a hotel per room and added costs of meal, you may find that the price of staying in a villa is pretty competitive. Last minute deals are also. When it comes to booking it’s also much better to go with villa agents (rather than the likes of airbnb), as you generally can find a price better matched minus the usual fees and get that true service catered to your every need.

title: Where to stay in Bali
excerpt: When it comes to exploring new destinations, Bali has many options. Regardless of whether you’re hoping to take a spiritual journey or immerse yourself in scuba diving or surfing, Bali offers something for everyone. Not only is there an environment that must be seen to be believed, but there are several options available when holidaying […]
body: When it comes to exploring new destinations, Bali has many options. Regardless of whether you’re hoping to take a spiritual journey or immerse yourself in scuba diving or surfing, Bali offers something for everyone. Not only is there an environment that must be seen to be believed, but there are several options available when holidaying in Bali that ensure the destination is perfect for all types of budgets.
The following is an overview of some of the more popular locations in Bali, as well as some of the experiences that can be enjoyed along the way.
Despite how appealing Canggu is, there will be those searching for a location in Bali that’s more suitable for families and nightlife and Seminyak is a perfect choice.
Although famed for its collection of boutique shops, there’s a lot more to unearth when visiting Seminyak.
Those searching for nightlife with a difference will fall in love with the Potato Head and KuDeTa beach clubs which feature famed DJs from all over the world.
As well as being able to enjoy unique beach clubs and luxury accommodation, there are plenty of other experiences for those choosing to stay in Seminyak for their holiday.

One of the most popular pastimes in Seminyak is surfing, and Seminyak beach is the perfect location. Although as popular as other beaches in Bali, the beach in Seminyak feels less crowded, allowing for more freedom when it comes to surfing.
If there is little in the way of waves when hitting the beach then fear not, as there are plenty of stalls where you can enjoy some refreshments.

Those looking to explore the waters of Seminyak in a different way may want to take advantage of the professional diving schools in the area, which offer immersive and memorable dives that can be enjoyed by newbies and experienced divers.

Seminyak often finds favour with those that love food thanks to the iconic KuDeTa, the best restaurant in Seminyak. As well as being treated to an authentic Balinese experience in relation to food, but it’s a beautiful location for enjoying the famous Bali sunset.
Those visiting Bali for the first time will want to find a town that offers a balance of life in Bali, and those new to Bali will find this in abundance when visiting Canggu.
When compared to other locations in Bali, Canggu can seem a little stripped-back, but this is what makes it the perfect town for those visiting Bali for the first time.
The lack of tourists ensures that those visiting Canggu experience the organic side of Bali without obstruction, be it the surfer beaches, voluptuous villas or immersive rice fields.
Despite not having as much as tourist traffic as other areas in Canggu doesn’t mean that there is a shortage of things to do.

Those looking to enjoy the environment in an exciting way can take advantage of local surf schools and enjoy Canggu in an original way, whereas those looking for some respite can enjoy a relaxing Indonesian head massage which is not only revitalising but also very affordable.

There is also plenty to experience when it comes to food and drink in Canggu. One of the most popular eateries in Canggu is Shady Shack, which offers a great choice of food that include smoothies and homemade cakes, but there are plenty of other restaurants that offer local dishes as well as an Indonesian take on global dishes.
Perfect for Those Looking to Explore Their Spiritual Side

The popularity of Bali can be attributed to how much choice there is. Just as many head to Bali for the immersive nightlife and sense of adventure, others are keen to embrace their spiritual side, and Ubud is the perfect destination to achieve this.
Ubud has been cited as one of the most beautiful places in the world, but this isn’t the only appeal to this bohemian haven, as there is plenty more on offer.
Locals can be seen immersed in prayer, while surrounding rice fields, temples and waterfalls offer a sense of tranquillity that’s just not possible with some destinations.
